pathophysiology_summary: Vitiligo is an autoimmune destruction of melanocytes producing well-demarcated, completely depigmented (chalk-white) macules in a symmetrical, often periorificial (mouth/eyes) and acral distribution, with leukotrichia (white hairs) in affected areas and entirely normal skin texture (no scaling, induration or inflammation). Stress can precede a flare. It is benign to physical health but has a major psychosocial impact, and here has precipitated an acute panic reaction, catastrophised as a 'flesh-eating'/infectious disease. Discriminators: complete chalk-white depigmentation with sharp borders and normal texture distinguishes it from tinea versicolor (mildly hypopigmented with fine branny scale, upper trunk) and pityriasis alba (poorly defined, mildly scaly, off-white patches on children's cheeks); and preserved sensation within the patches with no thickened nerves or an active erythematous border argues against tuberculoid leprosy. Management is authoritative compassionate reassurance and acute panic support, rigorous daily sun protection of the depigmented (melanin-poor) skin, screening for associated autoimmunity (especially thyroid), and routine dermatology options (topical calcineurin inhibitors/phototherapy) — with a mental-health safety-net for self-harm and a review safety-net if any mimic feature (numbness/scale) is present.

differential.evidence_basis: Symmetrical chalk-white sharply-demarcated smooth macules with leukotrichia.
Mildly hypopigmented with branny scale on the upper trunk — not chalk-white periorificial smooth patches.
Poorly defined, mildly scaly off-white cheeks in children — not sharp complete depigmentation.
Screen sensation/nerves — an anaesthetic patch with an active border would suggest leprosy.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Reassure only after confirming the chalk-white smooth symmetrical pattern and normal sensation; manage the acute panic and screen self-harm.

differential.evidence_basis: Classic vitiligo; normal sensation/no scale exclude the infective mimics.
Normal sensation and no thickened nerves/active border — remains a review safety-net if features change.
Working diagnosis: reassurance + panic support + sun protection + autoimmune screening + dermatology/psychological options; self-harm safety-net.

red_flags.description: Anaesthetic patch/thickened nerves/scaling suggesting an infective or other cause (e.g., leprosy)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would change the diagnosis/management; vitiligo has normal sensation and no scale.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A treatable infection (leprosy) or other cause could be missed.

red_flags.description: Acute panic with possible self-harm risk
red_flags.clinical_significance: Severe psychosocial distress needs screening and a mental-health safety-net.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Reassuring vitiligo without checking sensation/scale (missing leprosy/other) or screening self-harm.
Not addressing the acute panic and major quality-of-life impact.
Not screening for associated thyroid/autoimmune disease.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Reassure after screening; sun protection, autoimmune (thyroid) screening and dermatology/psychological options, and acute panic support; ED/crisis line for self-harm; review if any numbness/scale (mimic).
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Reassuring without sensation/scale screen, or missing self-harm risk.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Vitiligo: reassurance and sun protection; screen for associated autoimmune disease (esp. thyroid); consider topical calcineurin inhibitors/phototherapy via dermatology; address psychosocial impact.
